name: Stained Glass Candy
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Lily white syrup (or Karo - must be colorless)
Approximately 10 cups Icing sugar (can be reused over and over)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the white sugar, lily white syrup, and water in a medium-sized pot.
2. Add the food coloring you want (green for spearmint, orange for orange) and bring to a boil.
3. Continue boiling until the candy thermometer reaches 300°F degrees (can take up to 20 minutes). Stir gently to mix all ingredients; once it starts to boil, do not touch.
4. Ensure the pot is large enough to allow for expansion as it boils.
5. While the candy mixture is boiling, shape a large piece of tin foil into a cookie sheet-type shape by folding up the sides and ends (about 3 feet long).
6. Sprinkle the foil with icing sugar to completely cover it and bank up the sides with icing sugar.
7. When the candy mixture reaches 300°F, remove from heat. Let sit for about 10 seconds and stir slightly to cool down.
8. Add about 1 to 1-1/2 teaspoons of oil-based flavoring depending on how strong you want the flavor. Stir until mixed (may bubble a bit; do not stand over the pot as flavor can escape in the air and up your nose).
9. Once the flavoring is combined, pour the mixture onto the icing sugar using a rubber spatula to scrape all the mixture from the pot.
10. Place the pot, spatula, and candy thermometer into a sinkful of hot soapy water.
11. As the candy cools, keep checking by trying to lift the edges. Once it reaches the consistency that you can lift a little, start cutting quickly.
12. Cut off chunks and then cut them up into bite-sized pieces. The outside will cool first; if it hardens too quickly, it may shatter like glass.
13. Continue working around until all is cut.
14. Place the candy in a strainer sitting over a bowl, shake off excess icing sugar, and allow to cool thoroughly.
15. Pack in airtight containers (Zip-Loc Freezer bags work well).
16. Once you have completed all desired flavors, place about 10 of each flavor into smaller zip-loc bags and seal them.
17. These make great Christmas gifts for family members and various people such as mail carriers, garbage collectors, paperboys, school teachers, and kids' friends.
